Circuit readiness · AP/WLC migration
Field Mission · Project Fastblue · ID: 112654
CLIENT: HONEYWELL · VIA FASTBLUE · EMEA
A client that called Orange
"a red flag."
We turned it into a success story.
Multi-country connectivity failures. Billing happening with no service delivered. Customer sentiment severely negative — Orange was considered unresponsive and operationally weak.
🇮🇹
Italy
IT1B
🇫🇷
France
FR3Q
🇸🇪
Sweden
SW58
IT1B · ITALY
No HOTO, No Support
Sites completed without formal HOTO. Customer complained of slow internet. Support responsibility was unclear — no one was accountable.
FR3Q · FRANCE
Months-Long Dispute
Circuit delivered but connectivity dispute unresolved for months. Customer stuck in limbo with no clear escalation path or resolution in sight.
SW58 · SWEDEN
Billed, Never Installed
Circuit was closed on GOLD and billing was running — but the physical installation never happened. Service billed with zero delivery.
HOW WE FIXED IT
HOTO process alignment
Escalated internally and located the correct Brazil support team. Initiated the first structured HOTO phase. Summarised long SLA documents into two focused handover docs. Built automation to trigger HOTO submission to managers on completion.
France — IP & circuit resolved
Scheduled a live call with Honeywell, Fastblue & Orange engineer. Rapid engineer assignment and collaborative troubleshooting. Resolved in one week an issue that had been pending for months.
Sweden — billing & installation
Coordinated with LDM, Marcelo & carrier. Clarified in-house wiring responsibility. Secured customer agreement. Orange absorbed first site survey. Scheduled second survey with carrier for IHW.
THE OUTCOME
Blockers resolved across 3 countries
HOTO process now automated & monitored
Customer: "red flag" → "I'll happily take projects with Orange"
Honeywell leadership acknowledged Orange Egypt's initiative to their management
> mission_recovery: SUCCESS
LESSONS LEARNED
Identify HOTO managers & service desk before the project starts
Define exactly what the HOTO document requires upfront
Set a clear SLA & acceptance timeframe with service desk
FORMAL APPRECIATION · OSAMA KAMAL · HONEYWELL
From: Osama.Kamal@Honeywell.com → To: Management · CC: Ahmed El-Bourgy · Feb 20, 2026
Osama sent a formal appreciation email to Orange management after the recovery — copying Ahmed directly. Below are the highlights.
2/3
Sites completed
90%
3rd site progress
↑
Orange reputation
"I would like to highlight the outstanding work delivered by Ahmed, which has significantly improved Orange's reputation from Honeywell's perspective."
"We initially had a clear direction to stop working with Orange; however, thanks to Ahmed's exceptional effort and unexpected push, we have now completed 2 out of 3 sites that had been stuck for months."
"We now find ourselves confidently showcasing to the aggregator that our collaboration with Orange is performing better than theirs."
THE SOLUTION · HOTO AUTOMATION OUTPUT
The automation built during recovery — HOTO package emails generated and sent directly to operations managers the moment a site is completed. Eliminates the manual handover failures that caused this crisis.
> automation_live
WHAT A DPM DOES UNDER PRESSURE
DIAGNOSE
Find root cause before it becomes a client call
STRUCTURE
Build a recovery plan everyone can follow
COMMUNICATE
Keep the client calm while the team fixes it

04 / Demo

Sample of My Work

Real automation tools I built and deployed at Orange Business — running in production and used by the team every day.

POWER AUTOMATE · SCHEDULED FLOW

Order Overdue Alert System

A fully automated flow monitoring open orders and sending personalised HTML alerts to each DPM when orders are overdue or approaching due date.

1
Recurrence trigger — runs on a scheduled cadence automatically
2
List rows from Excel — reads Open Orders table, pulling all active data
3
Apply to each — loops through every DPM and groups their overdue orders
4
Send from shared mailbox — dispatches personalised HTML report per recipient
> runs_in_production
EMAIL OUTPUT · HTML TEMPLATE

Personalised HTML Email Alert

The result of the flow — a branded HTML email sent to each DPM with a colour-coded table of their overdue or at-risk orders.

✓
Personalised greeting — name pulled dynamically from APM Names sheet
✓
Color-coded status — red for overdue, orange for due within 14 days
✓
Full order table — order number, customer, project, country, value, days overdue
✓
Action note — reminds DPM to coordinate with LDM/ODM and reset RTDD
> sent_from_shared_mailbox
OFFICE SCRIPTS · TYPESCRIPT

HTML Orders Script

A TypeScript Office Script reading Open Orders, processing each DPM's data, and generating the HTML body consumed by Power Automate.

function main(workbook: ExcelScript.Workbook) {
  const mainSheet = workbook.getWorksheet("Open Orders");
  const apmSheet = workbook.getWorksheet("APM Names");
  // Dynamic header detection — no hardcoded columns
  const idxTDD = mainHeaders.indexOf("Days to Due Date_BO");
  // Loops per APM, generates personalised HTML table
}
1
Dynamic headers — no hardcoded columns, resilient to sheet changes
2
Per-DPM grouping — separate HTML block per project manager
3
Output table — consumed directly by Power Automate
> typescript · office_scripts
EXCEL · SKF PROJECT · SEARCH TOOL

Site ID Search Tool

Custom-built Excel search tool for the SKF LAN Refresh project — type a Site ID and instantly retrieve everything: Gold Reference, MLAN, order status, contacts, IP details, and ready-made email subject lines.

✓
Enter Site ID — instant lookup across the full project dataset
✓
Full site profile — Gold Ref, MLAN, order status, IP, gateway, contacts, country
✓
Auto email subjects — generates subject lines for customer, FE dispatch, HOTO, CMDB & success notifications
✓
Used in production daily — EMEA team on the SKF project
> skf_project · daily_use
